series.title: [PATCH] target/loongarch: Add support for dbar hint variants

review.summary: The patch has a critical functional regression in `trans_dbar()`: it unconditionally emits a full SC barrier before decoding the hint, so every `dbar` still behaves as a full barrier and the new fine-grained handling never takes effect. There is also a compatibility bug for CPUs without `DBAR_HINTS`: reserved/no-op hints are returned as no-ops before the feature fallback check, contradicting the stated legacy behavior that all hints degrade to a full barrier.

stages.A: Patch intent is clear and the CPUCFG plumbing for `DBAR_HINTS` is straightforward.
stages.B: The translation change in `trans_dbar()` is not functionally correct as written because a full barrier is emitted unconditionally on entry.
stages.C: Legacy compatibility handling is inconsistent with the commit message and existing behavior for CPUs that do not advertise `DBAR_HINTS`.
stages.D: Build/configure impact appears minimal; the main concerns are runtime semantics and guest-visible ordering behavior.
stages.E: Recommendation: fix the unconditional barrier first, then reorder the fallback/no-op handling so non-`DBAR_HINTS` CPUs preserve full-barrier semantics for all hints.

findings.title: `trans_dbar()` always emits a full SC barrier before decoding the hint
findings.description: The first statement in the new `trans_dbar()` body is `tcg_gen_mb(TCG_BAR_SC | TCG_MO_ALL);`. That means every translated `dbar` gets a full barrier unconditionally, and the later hint decoding only adds an additional barrier or returns after the full one has already been emitted. As a result, the advertised fine-grained `dbar` hint support is not actually implemented; all hints still behave as full barriers.
findings.suggestion: Remove the unconditional `tcg_gen_mb(TCG_BAR_SC | TCG_MO_ALL);` at function entry and emit exactly one barrier only after the hint/fallback logic has selected the correct flags.
findings.confidence_reason: The regression is directly visible in the diff: the unconditional barrier executes on every path before any hint checks.

findings.title: Reserved/no-op hints bypass the non-`DBAR_HINTS` fallback and become no-ops on legacy CPUs
findings.description: The code returns early for hints `0xf` and `0x1f` before checking `avail_DBAR_HINT(ctx)`. The commit message explicitly says that when `cpucfg3.DBAR_HINTS = 0`, all `dbar` hints must fall back to a full barrier for compatibility. With the current ordering, those two hints become no-ops instead of full barriers on CPUs that do not advertise `DBAR_HINTS`, changing legacy guest-visible behavior.
patch_context: /* If the CPU does not support fine-grained hints,or for the special LL/SC
* loop barrier (0x700), emit a full barrier.
findings.suggestion: Check `!avail_DBAR_HINT(ctx)` before treating any hint as reserved/no-op, so legacy CPUs continue to translate every `dbar` as a full barrier. Only apply the `0xf`/`0x1f` no-op behavior when `DBAR_HINTS` is advertised.
findings.confidence_reason: This follows directly from the control flow in the patch and the compatibility contract stated in the commit message.
